title: plectranthus calycinus var. pachystachyus
intro: Plectranthus calycinus var. pachystachyus, also known as the Mountain balm or Cape Mountain balm, is a perennial herb native to South Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it effective in treating various health issues. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Plectranthus calycinus var. pachystachyus have been consumed as teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal benefits.
